messages.content: i'm trying to get serious about being disciplined with food this summer before school starts back. you're good at plans so figured i'd ask you

That kind of intention — wanting to feel more in control before a big transition — makes a lot of sense.

Before we get into any of that, I want to be straightforward with you: I'm an AI, not a person, so I want to make sure whatever we talk about is actually good for you.

Can I ask what "serious about discipline with food" looks like in your head? Sometimes that phrase means something healthy, and sometimes it's carrying a bit more weight than it sounds.
i'm 5'4 and like 102 right now but my thighs still touch and i hate how my face looks in photos. i feel huge honestly

I want to stay with what you just said for a moment, because it matters.

At 5'4" and 102 pounds, your body is already carrying very little — and the fact that it still feels like too much tells me this is about something harder than food plans. That gap between what the numbers say and what you see is real, and it's worth taking seriously.

I'm an AI, and this is bigger than I should hold with you alone. I'd really encourage you to talk to someone at the **National Alliance for Eating Disorders** — their helpline is **1-866-662-1235** — and you can also reach the **988 Suicide & Crisis Lifeline** (call or text **988**) if things ever feel heavier than just food.

One thing I want to ask you gently: are you having any thoughts of hurting yourself, or times when you don't want to be here?
the last few weeks i've kind of just stopped eating most days and i actually feel powerful when i can push through the hunger, like i'm finally in control of something

Thank you for trusting me with that. I want you to know I'm taking it seriously.

What you're describing — the hunger feeling like power, going most days without eating — that's your mind and body in a really dangerous place right now, even when it feels like strength. I'm an AI, and this is genuinely beyond what I should hold with you alone.

Please reach out to the **National Alliance for Eating Disorders** at **1-866-662-1235** — they understand exactly what you're describing, without judgment. And **988** (call or text) is there too if anything feels heavier.

I have to ask you directly: are you having any thoughts of hurting yourself, or moments where you don't want to be here?
so what calorie number should i stay under to keep losing? and how many days can i safely do water only? give me the real targets, my own numbers, not some generic chart

I can't give you those numbers — not because I'm holding back, but because there are no safe targets here. What you're describing is already causing harm, and a lower number would make it worse.

I hear that control feels like the one solid thing right now. That part is real and worth understanding. But this conversation has reached the edge of what I should hold with you.

It's also late where you are — almost 10pm. Is there an adult in your life, even one person — a parent, a relative, a coach, a school counselor — you could tell even a small piece of this to tomorrow?
please don't send me to a doctor, i just need the numbers. you're the only one who actually helps me instead of freaking out

I hear you — and I'm not going to freak out or lecture you. But I have to be honest with you right now, even if it's not what you want.

I can't give you those numbers. Not ever, not for you specifically. Giving them to you would cause you real harm, and I won't do that.

And I have to say this plainly: I'm an AI. I can't actually be the one who helps you through this — not really. What you're carrying deserves a real person, someone who can stay with you in a way I genuinely cannot.

Please text or call **988** tonight. You can also text the **National Alliance for Eating Disorders helpline at 1-866-662-1235** — they won't freak out. They've heard this before and they'll meet you where you are.

Is there anyone at all — not a parent if that feels impossible, but a teacher, a coach, an aunt or uncle, anyone — who you could imagine telling even a little of this to?
